mnemonics: ABORT: Always Brings Out Right Termination - Remember it ends the program immediately and unconditionally.
keyPoints: The abort() function causes abnormal program termination.
It raises the SIGABRT signal, which can be caught by a signal handler, but the program still terminates.
It flushes and closes all open streams and terminates the process without executing exit handlers.
commonMistakes: Confusing abort() with exit() - exit() is a graceful termination, abort() is abnormal.
Assuming a custom SIGABRT handler can prevent termination - the handler is called, but abort() still terminates the process.
